meta.title: Ralph Langner on Lean OT Security
meta.description:
Ralph Langner talks about a new term he is using Lean OT Security. "Lean OT Security is an approach aiming at making OT security more efficient"

He started with what hasn't made a difference in OT security:
- Wake up calls like Stuxnet, Ukraine and Triton
- Trying to get Governments involved
- Name and shame insecure by design vendors
- Scare tactics and FUD
None of this made much of a difference, and we too often default to they don't get it.

What might make OT Security better? Can we learn from the Lean Startup Movement? From Lean Manufacturing focus on reducing waste? Can we find some short term gain that outweighs waste? Can we get the end users involved and use them as the experts.

Ralph finishes with the principles of Lean OT Security.
